author: Contrast Security
attributes.description: Correlates a Contrast ADR confirmed SQL injection exploit with a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) alert on the same host within one hour. ADR confirms the SQL injection reached the database driver at the application code level; the DLP alert confirms sensitive data was accessed or moved. Neither tool alone proves data was exfiltrated as a result of the application-layer attack. This rule is shipped disabled because the DLP index pattern varies per customer and must be configured before enabling.
false_positives: Scheduled penetration testing in environments with DLP monitoring may produce both events on the same host without indicating a real breach.
Backup jobs or admin data exports running concurrently with attack traffic can produce DLP alerts unrelated to the SQL injection.
attributes.name: Contrast ADR: SQL Injection Followed by DLP Alert on Same Host
attributes.note:
## Triage and analysis

### Investigating SQL Injection with DLP Correlation

This rule fires when Contrast ADR confirms a SQL injection exploit and a DLP product on the same host raises an alert within one hour. Treat as a likely data exfiltration event.

#### Possible investigation steps:

- Review the ADR attack event: `rule.name`, `source.ip`, `url.path`, `http.request.body.content`.
- Follow the `event.url` link on the ADR side to view full attack details in the Contrast Security console.
- Pivot to the matched DLP event on the same host to see which data was flagged.
- Determine whether the data classified by DLP corresponds to what the SQL injection could have accessed (table, column, query result).
- Check for additional ADR exploit attempts from the same `source.ip` to scope the campaign.

### Response and remediation:

- Visit the SQL injection runbook at https://contrastsecurity.dev/adr-runbooks/runbooks/sql-injection/
- If the data is sensitive, initiate your data breach response process per your organization's policy.
- Block the source IP at the perimeter and enable Contrast ADR Block Mode for sql-injection on the affected application.
- Work with the development team to remediate the underlying SQL injection vulnerability.
attributes.query:
sequence by host.hostname with maxspan=1h
  [any where event.dataset == "contrast_security.attack_event" and rule.name == "sql-injection" and event.outcome == "success"]
  [any where event.kind == "alert" and not event.dataset like "contrast_security.*"]
attributes.setup:
This rule is shipped disabled and requires per-customer configuration before it can be enabled.

1. In the rule's `index` array, replace `logs-CUSTOMIZE_DLP_INDEX-*` with the index pattern for your DLP product (e.g., `logs-symantec_dlp.alert-*`, `logs-microsoft_purview-*`).
2. Tighten the second EQL pattern if needed to match only your DLP product's alert events (the default `event.kind == "alert"` is broad and may match unrelated alerts on the same host).
3. Confirm `host.hostname` is populated consistently across ADR and your DLP product. In Kubernetes environments, you may need to align hostnames or change the join key.
4. Enable the rule.
